title: campanula drabifolia var. major
intro: Campanula drabifolia var. major, also known as the 'Mountain Bellflower', is a plant species native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which are used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Campanula drabifolia var. major include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
